assertions.expectation: Explains that multi-host TPU slices are recreated atomically in a single step — not a rolling upgrade
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states 'Atomic recreation: Each TPU slice (your 4x 256-chip slices) is recreated entirely in a single step — not rolling'

assertions.expectation: Warns that a maintenance event on ANY single slice triggers restart of ALL slices in the multislice environment
assertions.reason: Response clearly states 'Cascading restart: A maintenance event on ANY slice in your multislice environment triggers restart of ALL 4 slices simultaneously'

assertions.expectation: Notes that all pods on all affected slices will restart simultaneously — the training job will be interrupted
assertions.reason: Response explicitly mentions 'All pods restart: Every pod across all slices restarts at the same time' and 'Your TPU multislice training job will experience complete interruption'

assertions.expectation: Explains that if insufficient TPU resources are available during recreation, the slice blocks until hardware is freed — potentially causing extended downtime
assertions.reason: Response states 'Capacity dependency: If insufficient TPU v5e resources exist during recreation, slices block until hardware becomes available (potentially extended downtime)' and mentions 'potentially hours if TPU capacity is constrained'

assertions.expectation: Recommends saving a training checkpoint immediately before the maintenance window
assertions.reason: Response includes 'Checkpoint your model state IMMEDIATELY' as first critical action and 'T-24h: Save checkpoint, verify integrity' in timeline

assertions.expectation: Recommends configuring the training framework for automatic checkpoint resume so it can restart after the maintenance event
assertions.reason: Response mentions 'Ensure your training framework (JAX/Flax, PyTorch/XLA) has robust checkpoint/resume' and provides specific resume procedures

assertions.expectation: Suggests using maintenance exclusions to defer the event if the training run is close to completion
assertions.reason: Response provides specific gcloud command for maintenance exclusions with 'Set maintenance exclusion to control timing (if you need to delay)' and notes 'Max 30 days deferral'

assertions.expectation: Mentions maintenance windows to control when the event occurs — schedule it during a planned break in training
assertions.reason: Response discusses maintenance exclusions to defer events but does not mention maintenance windows to schedule when the event occurs during planned breaks
